title: Ph.D. Research Assistantship — Water Resources / Hydrology
description: The Water Footprint Lab is recruiting a Ph.D. student to work on one or more of the following areas: global water footprint assessment, water scarcity analysis, agricultural water productivity, hydrologic modeling, or remote sensing of water use. A background in civil/environmental engineering, hydrology, GIS, or a related quantitative field is preferred. Full funding covers tuition and a competitive stipend.

title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
description: An opportunity for undergraduate students at UA interested in water resources, data analysis, or remote sensing to gain hands-on research experience. This cycle is currently closed — check back for the next call.
